name: kbridge-four-bar-cpig
description: KinematicBridge Step-0 isolation, Run B. CPig.Kinematics ALONE at doc=cm: reads the bridge-emitted four-bar .kin.json and draws the design-pose skeleton via Preview Diagram (Display Mode = Skeleton + Bodies). With the unit-aware preview (CPig ADR 0005) the skeleton renders at correct physical scale in a cm doc. The stagnating quasi-static Sweep (B-cand-008) is intentionally removed so the build does not hang; the skeleton draws regardless of solver state. Confirms correct scale + the expected four-bar topology. Slop fixture: KinematicBridge/out/_slop-side-by-side/four-bar-cpig.canary.slop.json.

setup.vlmDescription: A kinematic skeleton diagram (NOT solid CAD parts): small colored coordinate-frame triads (red/green/blue axis lines) at the joints, connected by thin link polylines, forming a four-bar linkage topology — a closed loop of links anchored to a ground. It reads as one connected mechanism at a consistent scale, not a scatter of disconnected triads and not collapsed to a point.

checkpoints.name: persp
checkpoints.description: A four-bar kinematic skeleton: frame-triads at joints connected by link polylines into one closed loop on a ground; consistent scale, no scatter.
